DISCOVERY 612, NTC AND MTN FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST PLTFS FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY REQUESTS - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT KURT REITMAN'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFFS FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR. THE NOTICE WAS INSUFFICIENT PER C.C.P. SECTIONS 1005 AND 1013. THE MOTION MUST BE FILED AND SERVED 16 COURT DAYS PRIOR TO THE HEARING DATE PLUS 5 DAYS FOR MAILING. THIS MOTION WAS FILED ON AUGUST 7, 2009. THE FIRST DAY THIS MOTION COULD HAVE BEEN HEARD IS SEPTEMBER 8, 2009. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

DISCOVERY 612, DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR ISSUE AND EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFFS FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY REQUESTS IS GRANTED AS IT SEEKS TERMINATING SANCTIONS. NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED. THE COURT FINDS THAT ADDITIONAL ORDERS TO RESPOND TO DISCOVERY WOULD NOT RESULT IN COMPLIANCE. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion For Terminating, Or In The Alternative For Issue And Evidentiary, Sanctions Against Pltfs For Failure To Comply With Order Compelling Further Responses To Discovery Requests
Off Calendar Oct-06-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Defendant's Motion For Terminating, or in the alternative, For Issue And Evidentiary Sanctions Against Plaintiffs For Failure To Comply With Order Compelling Further Responses To Discovery Requests is granted as it seeks terminating sanctions. No opposition has been filed. The court finds that additional orders to respond to discovery would not result in compliance. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED. (EAH/612)

Ntc And Mtn For Sanctions Against Pltfs For Failure To Comply With Order Compelling Further Responses To Discovery Requests
Off Calendar Sep-03-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Gina Sharron, Atty. for defendant and moving party: Kurt Reitman
No appearances for Plaintiff.

THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Defendant Kurt Reitman's Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiffs For Failure To Comply With Order Compelling Further Responses To Discovery is ordered off calendar. The notice was insufficient per C.C.P. Sections 1005 and 1013. The motion must be filed and served 16 court days prior to the hearing date plus 5 days for mailing. This motion was filed on August 7, 2009. The first day this motion could have been heard is September 8, 2009. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery Requests And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Defendant Kurt Reitman's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery Requests is granted. No opposition has been filed. Plaintiffs' failure to respond fully was a misuse of the discovery process. Plaintiffs are ordered to respond further within 15 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiffs in the amount of $915, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.
